name: The Perfect Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the shortening in a small plastic food bag.
2. Flatten the shortening between plastic sheets and return to freezer.
3. This allows the shortening to become hard enough to break into tiny pieces when added to dry ingredients.
4. Tear two pieces of wax paper about 15 inches long and place on a counter.
5. Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, and salt onto the wax paper.
6. Place an empty sifter on top of a bare sheet of wax paper.
7. Lift the sheet of sifted dry ingredients by its sides and pour through the sifter, sifting onto the bare wax paper.
8. Sift back and forth three times, then sift once more into a large mixing bowl.
9. Remove the shortening from the freezer.
10. Cut into small bits, about 1/4-inch square.
11. Drop the shortening bits into the bowl of dry ingredients and mix with fingertips, occasionally tossing the flour mixture so you touch all particles of shortening with flour.
12. When the mixture has bits of flour-covered shortening throughout, begin adding buttermilk using a fork.
13. Mix lightly until combined.
14. Cover the board or surface with a dusting of flour.
15. Gather the sticky mass of dough and place on a floured surface.
16. Dust hands with flour and gently knead the dough, adding enough flour only to make it manageable.
17. Pat the dough into a round shape that is 1/2-inch thick.
18. Using a 2-inch cutter, cut out biscuits and place them touching each other in three rows on a greased baking sheet.
19. Place in the middle rack of a 425-degree oven and bake for 12 minutes, or until lightly golden.
20. Remove from oven and serve hot or warm. Makes 19 (2-inch round) biscuits.
